Summarized the Workout.md product spec: a minimal-friction workout tracker with tracking-first structured inputs, Markdown as storage, AI as a planning layer, deviation absorption, configurable coach, and no gamification.
how do you imagine the app? high level

Described a high-level app vision: one screen you live in during a workout (Today), with plan/calendar and coach-config surfaces behind it, AI as invisible ghost targets and inline nudges rather than a chatbot, and Markdown as the portable substrate never shown as the UI.

instead of a notes button, let me swipe right on the screen to go to a notes screen -- just like on tiktok you can swipe right to go to someone's profile -- same idea. But the notes shouldn't just be a notes, it should be a note but it should run the agent, like maybe I write "this felt kinda weird, it hurt my back a little" and maybe the agent writes back "ok, try with 50% less weight and tell me how it feels" and it would adjust the next set's weight, or maybe the agent decides "ok, let's skip this one for a few sessions and ease back into it in two weeks" and makes notes and whatnot to change the program, etc. the log & next button we can remove since I would just swipe down to the next exercise when I'm done.

Reported all requested changes live and verified on the simulator: swipe-right Coach screen with agent-driven plan edits, removed Log & Next and Note buttons, effort RPE dial, and a shared observable workout session model.

A session-scoped Stop hook is now active with condition: "make it real, real features, real functionalities, use openrouter/ollama models via rig.rs, just like ~/Work/win app does. Allow configuring the providers/model for the agent and tools -- Provide a real settings and rich settings and other screens that would be necessary. Use an opus agent to determine when we have the real features ready to meet what the product needs, including agent memories, tools, screens, interface to send messages to the coach. Use PRs + merge code back to master. use sonnet agents for coding, haiku agents to test on the device. Deploy to my iphone when you are done. Ensure the app ends up polished and sleek, with everything a finished app needs. I want to be able to immediately sync my workouts to iCloud or, even better, as a private github repo that the app commits to constantly (wouldn't that be cool?!) like the user would signup with github, a new repo is created with their workouts and the app commits a new markdown file with the changes of each workday -- frequently the app pulls from github to sync up any changes the user might have done such that changes done outside the app can be ingested by the app, and reviewed by the agent (like the agent might see, oh, we have new commits, let's see if there's something I should take into account) it would also be very cool if it connected to ~/src/tenex-edge -- like the coach agent could join a nip29 channel the user configures for it, and I could have my weight app coach agent, my personal assistant agent, my training coach -- all sharing context over how I'm doing there, etc. (i.e. the coach agent would get its own nsec, its own kind:0, just like the one in ~/Work/win but instead of talking over kind:1 it would talk over kind:9 (since this is where tenex-edge is settling on) work iteratively, line up a big epic with all these things as subitems and work one by one through them all landing them, testing them, etc". Briefly acknowledge the goal, then immediately start (or continue) working toward it — treat the condition itself as your directive and do not pause to ask the user what to do. The hook will block stopping until the condition holds. It auto-clears once the condition is met — do not tell the user to run `/goal clear` after success; that's only for clearing a goal early.
Fixed a build issue, committed the merge, and launched on a dedicated simulator to confirm no CloudKit/schema crash and that real plans work.

Reported the full epic complete and live on the user's iPhone: real AI coach with rig.rs, 5 plan-mutating tools, cross-launch memory, real plans with editor and plan-repair, GitHub sync with per-workday Markdown commits, iCloud sync, tenex-edge NIP-29 kind:9 fabric coach, finished-app shell with onboarding and settings, and 13 PRs merged to master with Opus gate pass.

when the user is sliding to the next screen let's add a nice animation and haptic feedback showing that the user is about to say they did it. or you know what would be cool? if the bottom section had a single rounded button, the user slides it up with exactly the same motion as sliding to the next screen, perhaps it shouldn't even slide up, like what I imagine is: I slide up the screen to go to the next screen, but there's a horizontal slider too; if I slide the slider to the right that means I did it and I see an animation making the button turn to a checkmark like "I did the thing" basically. and if I slide it to the left it means I'm skipping it, and we can remove the Skip button which irks me. So basically the motion with my thumb would be either diagonal from center-bottom of the screen (where the slider button is at) to up+right meaning triggering both the sliding to the next screen and triggering sliding of the slider to the right, or to the left. If I just slide up without sliding in any direction that means I'm just going to checkout the next exercises but I'm not advancing the workout stage. Do you understand what I mean? Is that possible? Explain what I mean in your own words briefly.

Confirmed understanding: a single round slider thumb at center-bottom where vertical drag navigates screens, horizontal drag commits the verdict (right = done with checkmark animation, left = skip), diagonal does both at once, and straight-up just peeks ahead without logging — and offered to build an interactive HTML mock first.
